old_claim.note: Satellite Technology provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: NAVSTAR's space segment requires an orbiting satellite constellation to broadcast the ranging and navigation data from which users determine position and velocity.

source_shape.source_locator: Report pp. 1-3, System Description and Space Segment: defines GPS as space-based and specifies a constellation of satellites continuously broadcasting navigation signals.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The contemporaneous GAO program report defines orbiting signal-transmitting satellites as GPS's complete space segment and as the ranging references used by receivers.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Removing artificial satellites would eliminate the broadcast geometry that distinguishes NAVSTAR from terrestrial radio navigation, not merely one optional implementation choice.

ontology_before: Satellite technology was treated as one optional enabling route to GPS.
ontology_after: An orbiting satellite space segment is a required constituent of the scoped NAVSTAR GPS architecture.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: satellite_technology remains required for gps.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to a terrestrial positioning system that does not use orbiting transmitters.
short_reason: The 1977 GAO architecture makes the transmitting satellite constellation constitutive of NAVSTAR GPS.
